claim: Vowel patterns containing the substring 'eo' (the 'eo family') fire at elevated rates on preparation-content folios (pharmaceutical + recipes) compared to non-preparation non-plant folios, within EACH scribal hand separately. If true, the 'eo' element is a cross-hand meta-feature for preparation vocabulary, not a hand-specific quirk.
evidence_for: Observational: Hand A pharmaceutical top markers (_.o.eo, o.eo, o.eeo, _.eeo) share 'eo' element
Observational: Hand B recipes top markers (o.eeo, o._.eeo, _.o.eeo) share 'eo' element
Across-hand parallelism: the observed pattern suggests a shared meta-vocabulary
evidence_against: Post-hoc pattern detection from the dialect dictionary cycle — this is why explicit pre-registration matters
'eo' element could simply reflect a high base rate of 'eo'-containing patterns across all sections
Sample is small: Hand A pharma = 16 folios, Hand B recipes = 23 folios, one preparation group per hand
test: Within each hand (A, B) separately: compare 'eo-family rate' (fraction of tokens whose vowel pattern contains the substring 'eo') on preparation folios (pharma + recipes in that hand) vs non-preparation non-plant folios (biological + cosmological + astronomical + zodiac + text-only + non-plant herbal in that hand). One-tailed Welch's t-test per hand, folio-level rates. Alpha = 0.05. Cohen's d >= 0.4. Mann-Whitney U one-tailed as non-parametric sanity. Bootstrap 95% CI on mean difference.
test_threshold: Both hands pass (p<0.05 AND d>=0.4): confirmed cross-hand meta-feature. Only one hand passes: PARTIAL (hand-specific). Neither passes: refuted.
tests_remaining: Execute the locked test in a separate commit after this pre-registration
Report per-hand numbers plus combined interpretation
reasoning: Explicit pre-registration of the cross-hand 'eo' family hypothesis before any data is inspected. The claim is a shared-meta-feature hypothesis: even with different hand dialects, both hands should use 'eo'-containing patterns more for preparation content. If the claim holds only for one hand, that's hand-specific not meta-shared.
